articles.title: Ukrainian Attacks Spark Fires At Oil Facilities In Russia, Crimea
articles.content:
基辅，乌克兰（美联社）——乌克兰和俄罗斯官员周一表示，乌克兰军队袭击了俄罗斯和被占领的乌克兰境内的石油设施，这是其让莫斯科为战争付出经济代价行动的一部分。

另外，乌克兰总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基证实，罗曼·阿布拉莫维奇充当了基辅与莫斯科之间信息传递的中间人。泽连斯基告诉天空新闻台，这位前英超球队切尔西的老板曾带着俄罗斯总统普京的信息前往基辅。

泽连斯基表示，阿布拉莫维奇传达的信息是，俄方“希望了解我们愿意做什么”，并提出可将回复带给普京。

同时，欧盟外交政策负责人表示，新一轮拟议的对俄制裁措施包括针对俄罗斯“军工复合体、侵犯人权者和宣传人员”的80个实体。

欧盟外交与安全政策高级代表卡娅·卡拉斯在周一欧盟防长会议后的新闻发布会上表示，西方制裁已使莫斯科损失约1.2至1.5万亿美元。

俄罗斯国防部表示，其军队在周一夜间击落了310架乌克兰无人机，其中包括莫斯科地区、俄罗斯西部和西南部、俄占克里米亚以及黑海和亚速海地区。

据乌克兰空军称，俄罗斯向乌克兰投放了155架无人机，其中124架被乌克兰防空部队击落或干扰。

乌克兰袭击俄能源设施

乌克兰总参谋部表示，乌军夜间袭击了俄罗斯克拉斯诺达尔边疆区，打击了靠近新罗西斯克的格鲁绍瓦亚石油转运基地。该设施是俄罗斯南部最大的石油和石油产品转运枢纽之一。

俄罗斯地方当局确认，乌克兰无人机在该设施引发火灾，但未造成人员伤亡。虽然他们未说明损失程度，但表示有130名救援人员参与灭火。

当被问及克里米亚是否面临燃料危机时，克里姆林宫发言人德米特里·佩斯科夫表示，能源部及其他机构正在制定一系列应对措施。

“目前确实存在一些问题，”佩斯科夫表示，“正在采取措施。”

总参谋部还表示，伏尔加格勒地区的克拉斯内亚尔“线性生产调度站”也遭到袭击。声明称，该地发生火灾。俄罗斯州长安德烈·博恰罗夫未说明该设施的具体功能，但表示没有人员受伤。

乌克兰还于周日晚间在俄占克里米亚的塞米科列茨卡亚石油基地发动袭击，引发火灾。据Telegram上发布的声明，该基地用于储存供应俄军的燃料储备。

乌克兰总参谋部表示，乌军还袭击了克里米亚费奥多西亚附近的一处油库。

泽连斯基向普京传递信息

泽连斯基表示，他给普京的信息是，他愿意在任何时间、任何地点（除俄罗斯或白俄罗斯外）与普京会面，可以是双边会晤，也可以在美国总统唐纳德·特朗普和欧洲领导人参与的情况下进行。

但他表示，乌克兰不会放弃目前部分被俄罗斯占领的顿巴斯地区。

“这是关键信息。我说过，我们不会离开，也不会放弃我们的领土，”泽连斯基告诉天空新闻台。

普京上周表示，一名未具名的俄罗斯商人上月前往基辅与泽连斯基会面，听取了其关于举行个人会晤的提议。但这位俄罗斯领导人拒绝了会面提议，称其毫无意义。

无人机袭击平民

据该地区军事行政负责人伊万·费奥罗夫称，俄罗斯无人机袭击造成两人死亡，至少18人受伤，其中包括四名儿童（年龄分别为5岁、10岁、13岁和12岁），袭击发生在乌克兰中部扎波罗热地区，损坏了住宅建筑、车辆并摧毁了市场摊位。

在尼科波尔，俄罗斯袭击造成一名49岁妇女死亡，另有四人受伤，据国家紧急情况服务部门称。

该部门还报告称，袭击击中住宅建筑后，第聂伯罗彼得罗夫斯克地区有四人受伤。在敖德萨，一名俄罗斯无人机袭击公共汽车站造成三人受伤。

地区当局表示，俄罗斯无人机夜间袭击还造成哈尔科夫、敖德萨和切尔尼戈夫地区平民受伤，并损坏了建筑物和企业。

此外，一架乌克兰无人机夜间袭击了从莫斯科开往俄占克里米亚辛菲罗波尔的客运列车，造成司机受伤、司机助手死亡，克里米亚亲俄地区领导人谢尔盖·阿克肖诺夫周一清晨报告称。

阿克肖诺夫补充说，没有乘客受伤。但袭击发生后，克里米亚所有客运列车服务暂停，乘客被疏散，并提供替代巴士服务。俄罗斯运营商Grand Service Express当天清晨在Telegram上报告了这一情况。

___
关注美联社对乌克兰战争的报道，请访问 https://apnews.com/hub/russia-ukraine
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces struck oil facilities in Russia and occupied Ukraine, Ukrainian and Russian officials said Monday, as part of their campaign to make Moscow pay an economic cost for the war.

Separately, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has confirmed that Roman Abramovich acted as a go-between for messages between Kyiv and Moscow. Zelenskyy told Sky News that the former owner of Premier League team Chelsea traveled to Kyiv with a message from Russian President Vladimir Putin.

Advertisement

Zelenskyy said Abramovich brought the message that the Russians “want to understand what we are ready to do,” and had offered to take a reply to Putin.

Meanwhile, the European Union’s foreign policy chief said a new proposed round of sanctions against Russia includes 80 listings targeting Russia’s “military industrial complex, human rights violators and propagandists.”

Kaja Kallas told a news conference after a meeting of EU defense ministers Monday that Western sanctions have already cost Moscow an estimated $1.2 to 1.5 trillion.

Advertisement

Russia’s Defense Ministry said its forces shot down 310 Ukrainian drones overnight into Monday, including over the Moscow region, western and southwestern Russia, Russian-occupied Crimea and the Black and Azov seas.

Russia targeted Ukraine with 155 drones, of which Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 124, according to its air force.

Advertisement

Ukraine strikes Russian energy sites

Ukraine’s General Staff said Ukrainian forces had struck Russia’s Krasnodar Krai region overnight, hitting the Grushovaya oil transshipment base near Novorossiysk. The complex is one of the largest transshipment hubs in southern Russia for oil and petroleum products.

Russian regional authorities confirmed a Ukrainian drone sparked a fire at the facility, adding that there were no casualties. While they did not comment on the extent of damage, they said 130 rescue workers were involved in putting out the blaze.

Asked whether the Kremlin is worried about the fuel crisis in Crimea, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said the Energy Ministry and other agencies are working on a set of measures to respond to the situation.

Advertisement

“There are indeed certain problems at the moment,” Peskov said. “Measures are being taken.”

The Krasny Yar “linear production and dispatching station” in the Volgograd region was also hit, the General Staff said. A fire broke out at the site, according to the statement. Russian Gov. Andrei Bocharov didn’t specify what the facility produces, but said there were no injuries.

Ukraine also carried out strikes overnight in the Semykolodezkaya oil base in the Russian-occupied Crimean Peninsula on Sunday night, sparking a fire at the facility. The base is used to store fuel reserves supplying the Russian military, according to the statement posted on Telegram.

Ukrainian forces also struck an oil depot near Feodosia in Crimea, the General Staff said.

Advertisement

Zelenskyy sent message to Putin

Zelenskyy said his message was that he would meet Putin “any time” in any location other than Russia or Belarus, and either bilaterally or with U.S. President Donald Trump and European leaders.

But he said Ukraine would not surrender the Donbas region, currently part-occupied by Russia.

“It was the key message. I said we will not leave and we will not go out from our territory,” Zelenskyy told Sky News.

Putin said last week that a Russian businessman, who he didn’t identify, traveled to Kyiv last month and met with Zelenskyy to hear his offer of a personal meeting. The Russian leader rejected the idea of a meeting, saying he saw no point in it.

Advertisement

Drone strikes civilians

Two people were killed and at least 18 injured, including four children aged 5, 10, 13 and 12, by a Russian drone attack in the central Zaporizhzhia region that damaged residential buildings and vehicles and destroyed market kiosks, said the regional military administration head, Ivan Fedorov.

In Nikopol. a Russian attack killed a 49-year-old woman and injured four other people, according to the State Emergency Service.

The service also reported that four people were injured in the Dnipropetrovsk region when strikes hit residential buildings. In Odesa, three people were wounded after a Russian drone struck a public transport stop.

Advertisement

Russian drone strikes overnight also injured civilians and damaged buildings and businesses in the Kharkiv, Odesa and Chernihiv regions, regional authorities said.

Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone overnight struck a passenger train traveling from Moscow to Simferopol in occupied Crimea, injuring the driver and killing the driver’s assistant, Kremlin-installed regional leader Sergei Aksyonov reported early Monday.

Akysyonov added that no passengers were hurt. But all passenger train traffic in Crimea was halted following the attack, with passengers evacuated and replacement buses provided, Russian operator Grand Service Express reported on Telegram that same morning.

Advertisement

___

Follow the AP’s coverage of the war in Ukraine at https://apnews.com/hub/russia-ukraine

Related
ukraine russiadrone strikes

articles.title: U.S. Army Helicopter Crashes Near Strait Of Hormuz
articles.content:
迪拜消息——一架美国陆军阿帕奇攻击直升机在霍尔木兹海峡附近坠毁，总统唐纳德·特朗普表示机上两名飞行员“没事”。该海峡目前仍处于伊朗封锁之下。

事故原因目前尚不清楚。中东地区周一刚经历伊朗和以色列之间的交火，这是伊朗战争名义停火以来面临的最大考验。伊朗官方媒体援引外国报道承认了坠机事件，但未作详细说明。

自2月28日美国和以色列开始对伊朗发动袭击以来，战争已动摇全球经济，推高世界各地能源价格，并导致包括食品在内的许多基本物资价格上涨。官员们一直未能将4月份的停火协议转化为永久结束冲突的方案。

特朗普周一晚间在纽约看完NBA总决赛后，于肯尼迪国际机场对记者们确认了坠机事件。

“飞行员没事。是的，”特朗普说。“没有人受伤。我们明天会发布报告。但飞行员没事。”

《纽约时报》最先报道了这架美国陆军阿帕奇攻击直升机在海峡附近不明情况下坠毁。美国军方中央司令部和国防部尚未回应美联社的置评请求。

（美联社驻纽约记者米歇尔·L·普赖斯对此文有贡献）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A U.S. Army Apache attack helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, with President Donald Trump saying the two pilots abroad are “fine” after the incident involving the waterway that remains under a chokehold by Iran.

What caused the crash remained unclear Tuesday morning in the Middle East, which was still reeling after Iran and Israel exchanged fire the previous day in the biggest strain yet to the nominal ceasefire in the Iran war. Iranian state media, relying on foreign reporting, acknowledged the crash, without elaborating.

Advertisement

Since the U.S. and Israel began striking Iran on Feb. 28, the war has shaken the global economy, driven up energy prices around the world and made many basics, including food, more expensive. Officials have been unable to turn the April ceasefire into a deal to permanently end the conflict.

Trump, speaking to journalists at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York after watching the NBA Finals on Monday night, acknowledged the crash.

“The pilots are fine. Yeah,” Trump said. “Nobody injured. We are going to issue a report tomorrow. But the pilots are fine.”

Advertisement

The New York Times first reported that a U.S. Army Apache attack helicopter went down near the strait in unclear circumstances. The U.S. military’s Central Command and the Defense Department did not immediately respond to requests for comment from The Associated Press.

___

Associated Press writer Michelle L. Price in New York contributed to this report.

Related
Iran warU.S. ArmyStrait of Hormuz

articles.title: ICC Chief Prosecutor Suspended Over Sexual Misconduct Allegations Pending Decision By Oversight Body
articles.content:
海牙消息（美联社）——国际刑事法院深陷困境的首席检察官卡里姆·汗于周一晚间被停职，此前法院监督机构已将其移交纪律处分程序。

这场持续两年多的丑闻中，汗面临与一名女性助手发生性行为不端的指控。他始终否认有任何不当行为。

这位英国律师的命运最终将由国际刑事法院监督机构——缔约国大会决定，该机构将召开特别会议，决定汗是否能继续担任这一国际法院的职务。

缔约国大会主席团——法院监督机构的执行委员会——在周一声明中表示，评估依据包括“联合国内部监督事务厅（OIOS）开展的调查报告、相关证据、一组司法专家特设小组的意见以及书面陈述”。

据美联社看到的调查报告副本，联合国调查发现证据表明汗“在其办公室、私人住宅以及出差期间与该助手发生非自愿性接触”。然而，执行委员会挑选的三名法官组成的小组在对调查结果进行法律评估后认为，调查结论不够确定。

汗已于2025年5月暂时离职，等待调查结论。这一程序对该法院而言史无前例，缔约国大会不得不反复制定新规以应对这一特殊情况。

针对汗的指控早在两年多前就向法院独立监督机构报告。美联社调查显示，汗被指将这名在法院其他部门工作的女性调入他的办公室。此后，她经常出现在官方行程中。

文件显示，在一次出访中，汗曾要求她躺在酒店床上休息，然后“对她进行性触碰”。文件中列举的其他非自愿行为包括锁上办公室门、将手伸入她口袋。汗还被指多次要求她陪同度假。

只有缔约国大会有权将汗免职，这一决定需要在125个成员国的无记名投票中获得多数支持。罢免他需要63个国家投赞成票。

会议日期尚未确定，但大会表示将尽快召开。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties late Monday, after the court’s oversight body referred Karim Khan for disciplinary proceedings.

In a scandal that has dragged on for more than two years, Khan is facing allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide. He has steadfastly denied any wrongdoing.

Advertisement

A final decision on the fate of the British barrister is now up to the Assembly of States Parties, the body that oversees the ICC, which will hold a special session to decide if Khan can remain in his job at the global court.

The Bureau of the Assembly of States parties — the executive committee of the court’s oversight body — said in a statement on Monday that it based its assessment on “on the report of an investigation undertaken by the United Nations Office of Internal Oversight Services (OIOS), the underlying evidence, the advice of an ad hoc Panel of judicial experts, and written submissions.”

Advertisement

The U.N. investigation found evidence that Khan had “nonconsensual sexual contact with (the aide) in his office, at his private residence, and whilst on mission,” according to a copy of its report seen by The Associated Press. However, a three-judge panel selected by the executive committee for a legal assessment of the findings found that the investigation was not conclusive enough.

Khan had already temporarily stepped down in May 2025 pending the outcome of the investigation. The process is unprecedented for the ICC, and the Assembly of States Parties has had to repeatedly create new rules to accommodate the situation.

The allegations against Khan were first reported to the court’s independent watchdog more than two years ago. An AP investigation revealed that Khan was alleged to have seen the woman working in another ICC department and moved her into his office. She later became a regular presence on official trips, according to whistleblower documents.

Advertisement

On one foreign trip, Khan allegedly asked her to rest with him on a hotel bed and then “sexually touched her,” the documents said. Other alleged nonconsensual behavior cited in the documents included locking the door of his office and sticking his hand in her pocket. He also allegedly asked her several times to accompany him on a vacation.

Only the Assembly of States Parties has the authority to remove Khan from office, a move that would require a majority in a secret ballot of its 125 member states. Sixty-three countries would need to support a measure to remove him.

No date was immediately set for the session, but the assembly said it would be convened as soon as possible.

Advertisement

Need help? Visit RAINN’s National Sexual Assault Online Hotline or the National Sexual Violence Resource Center’s website.

Related
United Nationsnetherlandsinternational criminal courtICC Assembly States PartiesKarim Khan
